title: Investment Property Mortgages in Greater Vancouver
metaTitle: Investment Property Mortgage in BC — Up to 80% LTV
metaDescription: Mortgages for rental and investment properties in Greater Vancouver and across BC. Up to 80% LTV, rental income offsets, and structures for portfolio investors.
intro: Buying a rental in Greater Vancouver is one of the most common 'next moves' my clients make. The federal cap is 80% LTV (20% down minimum), and lender programs vary widely on how they treat rental income.

body.heading: Down payment and qualifying
paragraphs: Federally regulated lenders cap rental property mortgages at 80% loan-to-value — meaning at least 20% down. Some lenders push this to 75% on properties they consider higher-risk (small bachelor units, properties in less liquid markets). Credit unions occasionally offer 85% LTV on rentals through specific programs.

On qualifying, the big variable is rental income offset. The strongest lenders offset 50% of rental income against the new mortgage payment (i.e., subtract from your debt-service ratio rather than add to your income). Some 'rental add-back' lenders treat rental income as additional income — much more generous, often the difference between qualifying and not.

body.heading: Structuring a portfolio
paragraphs: Most A-lenders cap individual borrowers at 4–5 rental properties. Beyond that, you'll need to move to specialty lenders, potentially personal-guaranteed corporate-held structures, or commercial rental programs. Cross-collateralizing (using one property's equity to secure another mortgage) is occasionally useful but reduces flexibility — discuss before doing.

Tax treatment: rental income is taxable, mortgage interest is deductible against it. Consult an accountant — rental property structuring is one of the few areas where accountant-broker collaboration genuinely matters.

whoItsFor: Are buying your first rental and want it structured well
Already own 1–4 rentals and are planning your next
Want to graduate to corporate-held or commercial structures
requirements: 20% down payment minimum (preferably from non-rental savings)
Standard income, credit and ID documentation
Lease or proposed rental income for the new property
Schedule of any existing rental properties (T776 statements)

faqs.question: Can I use rental income to qualify?
faqs.answer: Yes — but how the lender treats rental income varies enormously. Some offset 50%, some add 50–80% to your income, some ignore it. The right lender match can mean qualifying for a $1.2M rental vs. an $800K rental on the same income.

faqs.question: Do I need a real estate corporation?
faqs.answer: Not for the first 1–3 rentals. Beyond that, corporate ownership starts to make tax and liability sense — though it complicates mortgage qualification and pricing. Talk to your accountant first.

faqs.question: What's the minimum credit score?
faqs.answer: A-lender rental programs typically want 680+. Credit unions and B-lenders go to 600. Below that, equity-based or private lender rental financing is possible at higher rates.
